## prov_yearSumatera Barat_2021       -2.55335     0.72463  -3.52365  0.00043
## prov_yearSumatera Barat_2022       -2.51580     0.71615  -3.51296  0.00044
## prov_yearSumatera Barat_2023       -2.36534     0.71908  -3.28940  0.00100
## prov_yearSumatera Barat_2024       -2.81952     0.73432  -3.83965  0.00012
## prov_yearSumatera Selatan_2015     -0.84768     0.70203  -1.20747  0.22725
## prov_yearSumatera Selatan_2016    -24.40031 22632.68634  -0.00108  0.99914
## prov_yearSumatera Selatan_2017     -2.31383     0.83297  -2.77780  0.00547
## prov_yearSumatera Selatan_2018     -1.02134     0.67902  -1.50413  0.13255
## prov_yearSumatera Selatan_2019     -1.27349     0.68319  -1.86403  0.06232
## prov_yearSumatera Selatan_2020     -1.11841     0.67163  -1.66523  0.09587
## prov_yearSumatera Selatan_2021     -1.12148     0.67294  -1.66653  0.09561
## prov_yearSumatera Selatan_2022     -0.90012     0.66848  -1.34651  0.17814
## prov_yearSumatera Selatan_2023     -1.09232     0.67876  -1.60929  0.10755
## prov_yearSumatera Selatan_2024     -1.10369     0.68574  -1.60949  0.10751
## prov_yearSumatera Utara_2015       -3.84769     0.96528  -3.98608  0.00007
## prov_yearSumatera Utara_2016       -2.64418     0.69894  -3.78313  0.00015
## prov_yearSumatera Utara_2017      -25.10910 16083.38769  -0.00156  0.99875
## prov_yearSumatera Utara_2018       -2.91633     0.68252  -4.27291  0.00002
## prov_yearSumatera Utara_2019       -1.65462     0.61546  -2.68841  0.00718
## prov_yearSumatera Utara_2020       -1.59065     0.61341  -2.59314  0.00951
## prov_yearSumatera Utara_2021       -1.87966     0.62055  -3.02903  0.00245
## prov_yearSumatera Utara_2022       -1.73751     0.62238  -2.79170  0.00524
## prov_yearSumatera Utara_2023       -1.89861     0.63331  -2.99789  0.00272
## prov_yearSumatera Utara_2024       -2.16045     0.64346  -3.35755  0.00079
## prov_yearYogyakarta_2015           -1.08603     0.99114  -1.09574  0.27319
## prov_yearYogyakarta_2016           -2.36699     1.17383  -2.01647  0.04375
## prov_yearYogyakarta_2017          -25.24019 39410.51047  -0.00064  0.99949
## prov_yearYogyakarta_2018           -1.26859     0.95450  -1.32906  0.18383
## prov_yearYogyakarta_2019           -1.34773     0.94709  -1.42302  0.15473
## prov_yearYogyakarta_2020           -1.08722     0.92820  -1.17132  0.24147
## prov_yearYogyakarta_2021           -1.26873     0.93399  -1.35840  0.17434
## prov_yearYogyakarta_2022           -1.40579     0.92893  -1.51335  0.13019
## prov_yearYogyakarta_2023           -1.43726     0.93256  -1.54119  0.12327
## prov_yearYogyakarta_2024           -1.05321     0.93138  -1.13080  0.25814
## national_daily                      0.10287     0.00110  93.84469  0.00000
## cross_lag                           7.85939     0.93579   8.39870  0.00000
cat("\nSmooth Terms:\n")
## 
## Smooth Terms:
print(round(summary(m1_best)$s.table, 3))
##                      edf Ref.df      F p-value
## s(admin2_factor) 368.168    436 16.022       0
cat("\n─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
## 
## ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
cat("Cross-District Contagion Effect:\n")
## Cross-District Contagion Effect:
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
## ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Cross-district contagion
alpha_est <- coef(m1_best)["cross_lag"]
se_est <- summary(m1_best)$p.table["cross_lag", "Std. Error"]
t_est <- alpha_est / se_est
p_est <- summary(m1_best)$p.table["cross_lag", "Pr(>|t|)"]

cat(sprintf("  Cross-district contagion (α_cross): %.4f (SE = %.4f, t = %.2f, p = %.2e)\n",
            alpha_est, se_est, t_est, p_est))
##   Cross-district contagion (α_cross): 7.8594 (SE = 0.9358, t = 8.40, p = 4.52e-17)
cat(sprintf("    exp(α_cross) = %.2f (%.0f%% increase per unit cross-district exposure)\n",
            exp(alpha_est), 100 * (exp(alpha_est) - 1)))
##     exp(α_cross) = 2589.95 (258895% increase per unit cross-district exposure)
cat("\n─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
## 
## ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
cat("Interpreting Normalized Exposure:\n")
## Interpreting Normalized Exposure:
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
## ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
cat("  Both spatial and temporal kernels are normalized:\n")
##   Both spatial and temporal kernels are normalized:
cat("  - Temporal weights sum to 1: g(1) + g(2) + ... + g(30) = 1\n")
##   - Temporal weights sum to 1: g(1) + g(2) + ... + g(30) = 1
cat("  - Spatial weights row-normalized: Σ_s w_rs = 1 for each r\n\n")
##   - Spatial weights row-normalized: Σ_s w_rs = 1 for each r
cat("  A '1 unit' increase in cross_lag means receiving the entire\n")
##   A '1 unit' increase in cross_lag means receiving the entire
cat("  weighted sum of neighbors' past protests (e.g., if all neighbors\n")
##   weighted sum of neighbors' past protests (e.g., if all neighbors
cat("  had 1 protest on average over the lag window).\n\n")
##   had 1 protest on average over the lag window).
cat("  In practice, typical non-zero cross_lag values are much smaller.\n")
##   In practice, typical non-zero cross_lag values are much smaller.
cat(sprintf("\nOptimal spatial decay: θ = %s\n",
            ifelse(is.infinite(theta_hat), "∞ (uniform)", paste0(theta_hat, " km"))))
## 
## Optimal spatial decay: θ = 200 km
cat(sprintf("Optimal temporal decay: κ = %.2f\n", kappa_hat))
## Optimal temporal decay: κ = 0.85
cat(sprintf("Temporal half-life: %.1f days\n", half_life))
## Temporal half-life: 4.3 days

4.7 Temporal Lag Structure (Diagnostic)

This section provides a diagnostic check on the temporal structure by fitting separate coefficients for each week. This helps validate whether the geometric decay kernel (κ) is appropriate.

cat("\n╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗\n")
## 
## ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
cat("║  LAG STRUCTURE DIAGNOSTIC: WEEKLY COEFFICIENTS              ║\n")
## ║  LAG STRUCTURE DIAGNOSTIC: WEEKLY COEFFICIENTS              ║
cat("╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝\n\n")
## ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
cat("Note: The main model uses geometric decay (κ^ℓ). This diagnostic\n")
## Note: The main model uses geometric decay (κ^ℓ). This diagnostic
cat("fits separate weekly coefficients to check if decay is monotonic.\n\n")
## fits separate weekly coefficients to check if decay is monotonic.
# Compute weekly lag exposures using optimal theta
if (is.infinite(theta_hat)) {
  W_best <- (dist_matrix > 0) * 1
  diag(W_best) <- 0
} else {
  W_best <- exp(-dist_matrix / theta_hat)
  diag(W_best) <- 0
}
W_best_norm <- sweep(W_best, 1, pmax(rowSums(W_best), 1e-10), FUN = "/")

# Week 1: lags 1-7, Week 2: lags 8-14, Week 3: lags 15-21, Week 4: lags 22-30
week_exposures <- list(
  week1 = matrix(0, n_days, n_dist),
  week2 = matrix(0, n_days, n_dist),
  week3 = matrix(0, n_days, n_dist),
  week4 = matrix(0, n_days, n_dist)
)

for (lag in 1:30) {
  lagged <- rbind(matrix(0, lag, n_dist), count_matrix[1:(n_days - lag), ])
  lag_exp <- t(W_best_norm %*% t(lagged))
  if (lag <= 7) {
    week_exposures$week1 <- week_exposures$week1 + lag_exp
  } else if (lag <= 14) {
    week_exposures$week2 <- week_exposures$week2 + lag_exp
  } else if (lag <= 21) {
    week_exposures$week3 <- week_exposures$week3 + lag_exp
  } else {
    week_exposures$week4 <- week_exposures$week4 + lag_exp
  }
}

lag_df <- data.frame(
  date = rep(dates_vec, n_dist),
  admin2 = rep(colnames(count_matrix), each = n_days),
  week1 = as.vector(week_exposures$week1),
  week2 = as.vector(week_exposures$week2),
  week3 = as.vector(week_exposures$week3),
  week4 = as.vector(week_exposures$week4)
)

panel_lags <- panel_clean %>%
  left_join(lag_df, by = c("admin2", "date"))

m_lags <- bam(count ~ poverty_rate + cpi +
                s(admin2_factor, bs = "re") +
                prov_year +  # Province-year fixed effect
                national_daily +
                week1 + week2 + week3 + week4 +
                offset(log_pop),
              data = panel_lags,
              family = quasipoisson(link = "log"),
              discrete = TRUE)

cat("Cross-district effect by week:\n\n")
## Cross-district effect by week:
lag_coefs <- data.frame(
  Estimate = coef(m_lags)[paste0("week", 1:4)],
  Std.SE = summary(m_lags)$p.table[paste0("week", 1:4), "Std. Error"],
  t.value = summary(m_lags)$p.table[paste0("week", 1:4), "t value"]
)
print(round(lag_coefs, 4))
##       Estimate Std.SE t.value
## week1   1.1973 0.0951 12.5933
## week2  -0.3453 0.1411 -2.4480
## week3  -0.2787 0.1487 -1.8742
## week4  -0.1051 0.1201 -0.8753
cat(sprintf("\nSum of weekly coefficients (total effect): %.4f\n", sum(lag_coefs$Estimate)))
## 
## Sum of weekly coefficients (total effect): 0.4682

5 Step 7: Compute Contagion Multiplier and Endogenous Fraction

cat("\n")
cat("╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗\n")
## ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
cat("║   AVERAGE CONTAGION MULTIPLIER & ENDOGENOUS FRACTION                    ║\n")
## ║   AVERAGE CONTAGION MULTIPLIER & ENDOGENOUS FRACTION                    ║
cat("╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝\n\n")
## ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
cat("NOTE: The 'average contagion multiplier' computed below is NOT the Hawkes\n")
## NOTE: The 'average contagion multiplier' computed below is NOT the Hawkes
cat("branching ratio (expected offspring per event). With normalized spatial\n")
## branching ratio (expected offspring per event). With normalized spatial
cat("and temporal kernels, it represents an average proportional effect of\n")
## and temporal kernels, it represents an average proportional effect of
cat("one district's protests on neighbors' expected protest rates.\n\n")
## one district's protests on neighbors' expected protest rates.
alpha <- coef(m1_best)["cross_lag"]

cat(sprintf("Cross-district effect (α_cross): %.4f\n", alpha))
## Cross-district effect (α_cross): 7.8594
cat(sprintf("exp(α_cross) = %.3f\n\n", exp(alpha)))
## exp(α_cross) = 2589.946
# Endogenous fraction via counterfactual decomposition
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
## ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
cat("  ENDOGENOUS FRACTION: Counterfactual Decomposition\n")
##   ENDOGENOUS FRACTION: Counterfactual Decomposition
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")
## ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Background intensity: fit model without cross_lag
m_bg <- bam(count ~ poverty_rate + cpi +
              s(admin2_factor, bs = "re") +
              prov_year +  # Province-year fixed effect
              national_daily +
              offset(log_pop),
            data = panel_best,
            family = quasipoisson(link = "log"),
            discrete = TRUE)

lambda_bg <- predict(m_bg, type = "response")
lambda_full <- predict(m1_best, type = "response")
lambda_triggered <- lambda_full - lambda_bg

# Sum to get totals
total_observed <- sum(panel_best$count)
total_expected <- sum(lambda_full)
total_background <- sum(lambda_bg)
total_triggered <- sum(lambda_triggered)

endogenous_fraction <- total_triggered / total_expected

cat(sprintf("Total observed events: %d\n", total_observed))
## Total observed events: 8508
cat(sprintf("Total expected (full model): %.0f\n", total_expected))
## Total expected (full model): 8508
cat(sprintf("Background component: %.0f\n", total_background))
## Background component: 8508
cat(sprintf("Triggered component: %.0f\n", total_triggered))
## Triggered component: -0
cat(sprintf("\nENDOGENOUS FRACTION: %.1f%%\n", 100 * endogenous_fraction))
## 
## ENDOGENOUS FRACTION: -0.0%
# Contagion effect magnitude
cat("\n═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
## 
## ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
cat("  CONTAGION EFFECT MAGNITUDE\n")
##   CONTAGION EFFECT MAGNITUDE
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")
## ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
cat("Note: With normalized kernels (temporal sums to 1, spatial row-normalized),\n")
## Note: With normalized kernels (temporal sums to 1, spatial row-normalized),
cat("the cross_lag coefficient represents the proportional increase in expected\n")
## the cross_lag coefficient represents the proportional increase in expected
cat("protests per unit of normalized exposure from past protests elsewhere.\n\n")
## protests per unit of normalized exposure from past protests elsewhere.
# Build row-normalized spatial weight matrix for interpretation
if (is.infinite(theta_hat)) {
  W_interp <- (dist_matrix > 0) * 1
  diag(W_interp) <- 0
} else {
  W_interp <- exp(-dist_matrix / theta_hat)
  diag(W_interp) <- 0
}
W_interp_norm <- sweep(W_interp, 1, pmax(rowSums(W_interp), 1e-10), FUN = "/")

# Average λ per district (predict once on whole panel, then aggregate)
panel_best <- panel_best %>%
  mutate(lambda_hat = predict(m1_best, newdata = ., type = "response"))

avg_lambda_by_district <- panel_best %>%
  group_by(admin2) %>%
  summarise(avg_lambda = mean(lambda_hat), .groups = "drop")

avg_lambda_vec <- avg_lambda_by_district$avg_lambda[match(colnames(count_matrix),
                                                          avg_lambda_by_district$admin2)]
names(avg_lambda_vec) <- colnames(count_matrix)

# For each origin district r, compute effect propagation
# This is an approximation of how influence spreads, not a literal branching ratio
effect_by_origin <- numeric(n_dist)

for (r in 1:n_dist) {
  total <- 0
  for (s in 1:n_dist) {
    if (s == r) next
    w <- W_interp_norm[s, r]
    # Effect at s from normalized exposure originating at r
    effect_s <- avg_lambda_vec[s] * (exp(alpha * w) - 1)
    total <- total + effect_s
  }
  effect_by_origin[r] <- total
}

# Weight by event frequency
events_by_district <- panel_best %>%
  group_by(admin2) %>%
  summarise(n_events = sum(count), .groups = "drop")

event_weights <- events_by_district$n_events[match(colnames(count_matrix),
                                                    events_by_district$admin2)]
event_weights <- event_weights / sum(event_weights)

# Weighted average contagion multiplier (not a literal Hawkes branching ratio)
avg_contagion_multiplier <- sum(effect_by_origin * event_weights)

cat(sprintf("Average contagion multiplier: %.4f\n", avg_contagion_multiplier))
## Average contagion multiplier: 0.0461
cat("\nInterpretation: On average, a protest event in one district increases\n")
## 
## Interpretation: On average, a protest event in one district increases
cat("the expected protest rate in neighboring districts by this amount,\n")
## the expected protest rate in neighboring districts by this amount,
cat("weighted by spatial proximity and temporal decay.\n")
## weighted by spatial proximity and temporal decay.

6 Results Summary

cat("\n")
cat("╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗\n")
## ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
cat("║  SUMMARY                                                    ║\n")
## ║  SUMMARY                                                    ║
cat("╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝\n\n")
## ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
cat("┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n")
## ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
cat("│ Quantity                              │ Value                   │\n")
## │ Quantity                              │ Value                   │
cat("├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤\n")
## ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
cat(sprintf("│ Cross-district contagion (α_cross)    │ %.4f                  │\n", alpha))
## │ Cross-district contagion (α_cross)    │ 7.8594                  │
cat(sprintf("│ Multiplicative effect exp(α_cross)    │ %.2fx                   │\n", exp(alpha)))
## │ Multiplicative effect exp(α_cross)    │ 2589.95x                   │
cat(sprintf("│ Optimal spatial decay (θ)             │ %-23s │\n",
            ifelse(is.infinite(theta_hat), "∞ (uniform)", paste0(theta_hat, " km"))))
## │ Optimal spatial decay (θ)             │ 200 km                  │
cat(sprintf("│ Temporal decay (κ)                    │ %.2f                    │\n", kappa_hat))
## │ Temporal decay (κ)                    │ 0.85                    │
cat(sprintf("│ Temporal half-life                    │ %.1f days               │\n", half_life))
## │ Temporal half-life                    │ 4.3 days               │
cat(sprintf("│ Endogenous fraction                   │ %.1f%%                   │\n", 100 * endogenous_fraction))
## │ Endogenous fraction                   │ -0.0%                   │
cat(sprintf("│ Avg contagion effect                  │ %.4f                  │\n", avg_contagion_multiplier))
## │ Avg contagion effect                  │ 0.0461                  │
cat("└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n")
## └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
cat("\n")
cat("KEY FINDINGS:\n")
## KEY FINDINGS:
cat(sprintf("  • Cross-district contagion (α_cross): %.4f (SE = %.4f, p = %.2e)\n",
            coef(m1_best)["cross_lag"],
            summary(m1_best)$p.table["cross_lag", "Std. Error"],
            summary(m1_best)$p.table["cross_lag", "Pr(>|t|)"]))
##   • Cross-district contagion (α_cross): 7.8594 (SE = 0.9358, p = 4.52e-17)
cat(sprintf("  • %.1f%% of expected protests are attributed to cross-district contagion\n",
            100 * endogenous_fraction))
##   • -0.0% of expected protests are attributed to cross-district contagion
cat(sprintf("  • Temporal decay with half-life of %.1f days\n", half_life))
##   • Temporal decay with half-life of 4.3 days
cat("\n")
cat("MODEL SPECIFICATION:\n")
## MODEL SPECIFICATION:
cat(sprintf("  • District random intercepts (%d districts)\n",
            length(unique(panel_best$admin2))))
##   • District random intercepts (469 districts)
cat("  • National daily count controls for nationwide shocks\n")
##   • National daily count controls for nationwide shocks
cat("  • Population as offset for per-capita interpretation\n")
##   • Population as offset for per-capita interpretation
cat(sprintf("  • Spatial decay: θ = %s (row-normalized)\n", ifelse(is.infinite(theta_hat), "∞", paste0(theta_hat, " km"))))
##   • Spatial decay: θ = 200 km (row-normalized)
cat(sprintf("  • Temporal decay: κ = %.2f (normalized, half-life = %.1f days)\n", kappa_hat, half_life))
##   • Temporal decay: κ = 0.85 (normalized, half-life = 4.3 days)
cat("\n")
cat("LIMITATIONS & CAVEATS:\n")
## LIMITATIONS & CAVEATS:
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
## ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
cat("  1. ZERO-INFLATION/REPORTING BIAS:\n")
##   1. ZERO-INFLATION/REPORTING BIAS:
cat("     The model treats all zeros as 'no protests' but some may reflect\n")
##      The model treats all zeros as 'no protests' but some may reflect
cat("     poor data coverage or under-reporting in certain districts.\n")
##      poor data coverage or under-reporting in certain districts.
# Report zero distribution
n_zero_days <- sum(panel_best$count == 0)
n_total_days <- nrow(panel_best)
pct_zero <- 100 * n_zero_days / n_total_days
cat(sprintf("     Zero-count district-days: %d of %d (%.1f%%)\n\n",
            n_zero_days, n_total_days, pct_zero))
##      Zero-count district-days: 1900439 of 1908816 (99.6%)
cat("  2. POPULATION EXTRAPOLATION (2021-2024):\n")
##   2. POPULATION EXTRAPOLATION (2021-2024):
cat("     Population data available through 2020; 2021-2024 values are\n")
##      Population data available through 2020; 2021-2024 values are
cat("     linear extrapolations. COVID-19 may have affected population\n")
##      linear extrapolations. COVID-19 may have affected population
cat("     (migration, mortality) making extrapolation less reliable.\n")
##      (migration, mortality) making extrapolation less reliable.
cat("     Sensitivity: consider using 2020 population for all post-2020 years.\n\n")
##      Sensitivity: consider using 2020 population for all post-2020 years.
cat("  3. NATIONAL COUNT ENDOGENEITY:\n")
##   3. NATIONAL COUNT ENDOGENEITY:
cat("     The national_daily variable is contemporaneous, so\n")
##      The national_daily variable is contemporaneous, so
cat("     shocks hitting multiple districts simultaneously could still create\n")
##      shocks hitting multiple districts simultaneously could still create
cat("     spurious correlation.\n\n")
##      spurious correlation.
cat("  4. SPATIAL AUTOCORRELATION IN INFERENCE:\n")
##   4. SPATIAL AUTOCORRELATION IN INFERENCE:
cat("     See Moran's I diagnostics below. If significant, standard errors\n")
##      See Moran's I diagnostics below. If significant, standard errors
cat("     from quasipoisson F-tests may be understated.\n")
##      from quasipoisson F-tests may be understated.

7 Model Diagnostics

7.1 Spatial Autocorrelation in Residuals

Test whether model residuals exhibit spatial clustering using Moran’s I. If residuals show significant spatial autocorrelation, it suggests omitted spatially-correlated variables, misspecified spatial kernel, or that standard errors may be understated.

cat("\n╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗\n")
## 
## ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
cat("║  MORAN'S I: SPATIAL AUTOCORRELATION IN RESIDUALS            ║\n")
## ║  MORAN'S I: SPATIAL AUTOCORRELATION IN RESIDUALS            ║
cat("╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝\n\n")
## ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
# Extract Pearson residuals from final model
panel_best$resid <- residuals(m1_best, type = "pearson")

# Aggregate residuals to district level (mean residual per district)
district_resid <- panel_best %>%
  group_by(admin2) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_resid = mean(resid, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  left_join(district_covariates %>% select(admin2, lon, lat), by = "admin2") %>%
  filter(!is.na(lon), !is.na(lat))

cat("Districts with valid residuals:", nrow(district_resid), "\n\n")
## Districts with valid residuals: 472
# Create spatial coordinates matrix
coords <- as.matrix(district_resid[, c("lon", "lat")])

# Build k-nearest neighbors (k=5)
k <- 5
knn <- knearneigh(coords, k = k)
nb <- knn2nb(knn)

# Create row-standardized spatial weights
listw <- nb2listw(nb, style = "W")

cat("Spatial weights created (k =", k, "nearest neighbors, row-standardized)\n\n")
## Spatial weights created (k = 5 nearest neighbors, row-standardized)
# Run global Moran's I test
moran_result <- moran.test(district_resid$mean_resid, listw)

cat("Global Moran's I Test Results:\n")
## Global Moran's I Test Results:
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
## ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
cat(sprintf("  Moran's I statistic:  %.4f\n", moran_result$estimate["Moran I statistic"]))
##   Moran's I statistic:  0.0192
cat(sprintf("  Expected value:       %.4f\n", moran_result$estimate["Expectation"]))
##   Expected value:       -0.0021
cat(sprintf("  Variance:             %.6f\n", moran_result$estimate["Variance"]))
##   Variance:             0.000705
cat(sprintf("  Z-score:              %.3f\n", moran_result$statistic))
##   Z-score:              0.802
cat(sprintf("  p-value:              %.4f\n", moran_result$p.value))
##   p-value:              0.2113
cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n")
## ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Interpretation and inference caveat
moran_significant <- moran_result$p.value < 0.05

if (moran_significant) {
  cat("⚠ WARNING: Significant spatial autocorrelation detected (p < 0.05)\n")
  cat("  This suggests the model may not fully capture spatial structure.\n")
  cat("  INFERENCE CAVEAT: Standard errors may be understated due to residual\n")
  cat("  spatial clustering. F-tests using quasipoisson dispersion do not\n")
  cat("  account for this. Consider spatial HAC standard errors for robust inference.\n")
  cat("  Interpret p-values with caution.\n")
} else {
  cat("✓ Residuals appear spatially independent (p >= 0.05)\n")
  cat("  The contagion model adequately captures spatial dependence.\n")
}
## ✓ Residuals appear spatially independent (p >= 0.05)
##   The contagion model adequately captures spatial dependence.

7.2 Monthly Moran’s I Over Time

Examine how spatial autocorrelation in residuals varies over time to identify periods with unmodeled spatial structure.

# Compute monthly Moran's I
monthly_moran <- panel_best %>%
  mutate(year_month = format(date, "%Y-%m")) %>%
  group_by(year_month) %>%
  group_modify(~ {
    # Get district-level mean residuals for this month
    month_resid <- .x %>%
      group_by(admin2) %>%
      summarise(mean_resid = mean(resid, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
      left_join(district_covariates %>% select(admin2, lon, lat), by = "admin2") %>%
      filter(!is.na(lon), !is.na(lat), !is.na(mean_resid))

    # Need enough districts for k-nn
    if (nrow(month_resid) < k + 1) {
      return(data.frame(moran_i = NA, p_value = NA, n_districts = nrow(month_resid)))
    }

    # Compute Moran's I for this month
    coords_m <- as.matrix(month_resid[, c("lon", "lat")])
    knn_m <- knearneigh(coords_m, k = k)
    nb_m <- knn2nb(knn_m)
    listw_m <- nb2listw(nb_m, style = "W")

    moran_m <- tryCatch(
      moran.test(month_resid$mean_resid, listw_m),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )

    if (is.null(moran_m)) {
      return(data.frame(moran_i = NA, p_value = NA, n_districts = nrow(month_resid)))
    }

    data.frame(
      moran_i = moran_m$estimate["Moran I statistic"],
      p_value = moran_m$p.value,
      n_districts = nrow(month_resid)
    )
  }) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(date = as.Date(paste0(year_month, "-01")))

# Plot monthly Moran's I
ggplot(monthly_moran %>% filter(!is.na(moran_i)), aes(x = date, y = moran_i)) +
  geom_line(color = "#3498db", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(aes(color = p_value < 0.05), size = 2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("FALSE" = "#3498db", "TRUE" = "#e74c3c"),
    labels = c("Not significant", "Significant (p < 0.05)"),
    name = "Spatial autocorrelation"
  ) +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 year", date_labels = "%Y") +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Moran's I",
    title = "Monthly Spatial Autocorrelation in Model Residuals",
    subtitle = "Red points indicate months with significant clustering (p < 0.05)"
  ) +
  theme_presentation +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# Summary
n_sig <- sum(monthly_moran$p_value < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)
n_total <- sum(!is.na(monthly_moran$p_value))
cat(sprintf("\nMonthly summary: %d of %d months (%.1f%%) show significant spatial autocorrelation\n",
            n_sig, n_total, 100 * n_sig / n_total))
## 
## Monthly summary: 47 of 118 months (39.8%) show significant spatial autocorrelation

8 Figures

8.1 Figure 1: Temporal Distribution

daily_counts_plot <- protests_daily %>%
  group_by(date) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop")

ggplot(daily_counts_plot, aes(x = date, y = n)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#2c3e50", alpha = 0.7, width = 1) +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 year", date_labels = "%Y") +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0), limits = c(0, NA)) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Daily Protest Count",
       title = "Temporal Distribution of Indonesian Protests") +
  theme_presentation +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

8.2 Figure 2: 2D Profile Likelihood (θ vs κ)

# Prepare data for 2D heatmap
profile_2d <- results_2d %>%
  mutate(
    theta_label = ifelse(is.infinite(theta), "∞", as.character(theta)),
    theta_label = factor(theta_label,
                         levels = c("50", "100", "200", "300", "500", "750", "1000", "1500", "∞")),
    kappa_label = sprintf("%.2f", kappa),
    kappa_label = factor(kappa_label, levels = sprintf("%.2f", sort(unique(kappa)))),
    is_optimal = (theta == theta_hat & kappa == kappa_hat)
  )

# Find optimal point
opt_point <- profile_2d %>% filter(is_optimal)

ggplot(profile_2d, aes(x = theta_label, y = kappa_label, fill = deviance)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_point(data = opt_point, aes(x = theta_label, y = kappa_label),
             color = "#e74c3c", size = 6, shape = 4, stroke = 2) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "plasma", direction = -1,
                       name = "Deviance") +
  labs(
    x = expression(paste("Spatial Decay ", theta, " (km)")),
    y = expression(paste("Temporal Decay ", kappa)),
    title = "2D Profile Likelihood: Spatial vs Temporal Decay",
    subtitle = sprintf("Optimal: θ = %s, κ = %.2f (marked with ×)",
                       ifelse(is.infinite(theta_hat), "∞", theta_hat), kappa_hat)
  ) +
  theme_presentation +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

8.3 Figure 3: Temporal Decay Kernel

# Plot the normalized temporal decay function g(l) = kappa^l / sum(kappa^m)
lags <- 1:30
w_opt <- kappa_hat^lags
w_opt <- w_opt / sum(w_opt)
w_unif <- rep(1/length(lags), length(lags))

kernel_data <- data.frame(
  lag = lags,
  weight_optimal = w_opt,
  weight_uniform = w_unif
)

# Compute half-life position
if (kappa_hat < 1) {
  hl_lag <- half_life
} else {
  hl_lag <- NA
}

# Find the weight at half-life for annotation placement
hl_weight <- if (!is.na(hl_lag) && hl_lag <= 30) w_opt[round(hl_lag)] else NA

ggplot(kernel_data, aes(x = lag)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = weight_uniform), color = "#95a5a6", linewidth = 1,
            linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_line(aes(y = weight_optimal), color = "#e74c3c", linewidth = 1.5) +
  geom_point(aes(y = weight_optimal), color = "#e74c3c", size = 2) +
  {if (!is.na(hl_lag) && hl_lag <= 30)
    geom_vline(xintercept = hl_lag, color = "#3498db", linetype = "dotted", linewidth = 1)} +
  {if (!is.na(hl_lag) && hl_lag <= 30)
    annotate("text", x = hl_lag + 1, y = max(w_opt) * 0.6,
             label = sprintf("Half-life\n%.1f days", half_life),
             hjust = 0, size = 4, color = "#3498db")} +
  annotate("text", x = 25, y = max(w_unif) + 0.005, label = "Uniform",
           color = "#95a5a6", size = 4) +
  annotate("text", x = 8, y = w_opt[5] + 0.01,
           label = sprintf("Normalized geometric (κ = %.2f)", kappa_hat),
           color = "#e74c3c", size = 4) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(1, 7, 14, 21, 30),
                     labels = c("1", "7", "14", "21", "30")) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0.02, 0)) +
  labs(
    x = "Lag (days)",
    y = "Temporal Weight g(ℓ)",
    title = "Normalized Temporal Decay Kernel",
    subtitle = sprintf("g(ℓ) = κ^ℓ / Σκ^m, κ = %.2f, half-life = %.1f days (weights sum to 1)", kappa_hat, half_life)
  ) +
  theme_presentation

8.4 Figure 4: Lag Structure

lag_data <- data.frame(
  week = factor(c("Week 1\n(1-7)", "Week 2\n(8-14)", "Week 3\n(15-21)", "Week 4\n(22-30)"),
                levels = c("Week 1\n(1-7)", "Week 2\n(8-14)", "Week 3\n(15-21)", "Week 4\n(22-30)")),
  coef = lag_coefs[, 1],
  se = lag_coefs[, 2]
)

ggplot(lag_data, aes(x = week, y = coef)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#3498db", alpha = 0.8, width = 0.7) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = coef - 1.96*se, ymax = coef + 1.96*se),
                width = 0.25, linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  labs(
    x = "Lag Period",
    y = "Coefficient",
    title = "Cross-District Contagion Effect by Temporal Lag"
  ) +
  theme_presentation

8.5 Figure 5: Covariate Effects

coefs_plot <- coef(m1_best)
ses_plot <- summary(m1_best)$p.table[, "Std. Error"]

cov_df <- data.frame(
  variable = factor(c("Poverty Rate", "CPI", "National Daily"),
                    levels = c("Poverty Rate", "CPI", "National Daily")),
  estimate = c(coefs_plot["poverty_rate"], coefs_plot["cpi"], coefs_plot["national_daily"]),
  se = c(ses_plot["poverty_rate"], ses_plot["cpi"], ses_plot["national_daily"])
)

ggplot(cov_df, aes(x = variable, y = estimate)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#9b59b6", alpha = 0.8, width = 0.6) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = estimate - 1.96*se, ymax = estimate + 1.96*se),
                width = 0.2, linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Coefficient (log scale)",
       title = "Background Rate Covariate Effects",
       subtitle = "Population enters as offset (coefficient fixed at 1)") +
  theme_presentation

8.6 Figure 6: Spatial Kernel

theta_plot <- ifelse(is.infinite(theta_hat), 100, theta_hat)

distances <- seq(0, 500, by = 5)
kernel_df <- data.frame(
  distance = distances,
  weight = exp(-distances / theta_plot),
  weight_uniform = rep(1, length(distances))
)

ggplot(kernel_df, aes(x = distance)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = weight), color = "#e74c3c", linewidth = 1.5) +
  geom_line(aes(y = weight_uniform), color = "#95a5a6", linewidth = 1, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = theta_plot, color = "#e74c3c", linetype = "dotted") +
  annotate("text", x = theta_plot + 10, y = 0.5,
           label = paste0("θ = ", theta_plot, " km"),
           hjust = 0, size = 4, color = "#e74c3c") +
  annotate("text", x = 400, y = 0.95, label = "Uniform (no decay)",
           color = "#95a5a6", size = 4) +
  annotate("text", x = 300, y = 0.15, label = "Exponential decay",
           color = "#e74c3c", size = 4) +
  labs(
    x = "Distance (km)",
    y = "Weight",
    title = "Spatial Kernel: Exponential Decay vs. Uniform"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1.1), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  theme_presentation

9 Session Information

sessionInfo()
## R version 4.3.2 (2023-10-31)
## Platform: aarch64-apple-darwin20 (64-bit)
## Running under: macOS Monterey 12.7.6
## 
## Matrix products: default
## BLAS:   /Library/Frameworks/R.framework/Versions/4.3-arm64/Resources/lib/libRblas.0.dylib 
## LAPACK: /Library/Frameworks/R.framework/Versions/4.3-arm64/Resources/lib/libRlapack.dylib;  LAPACK version 3.11.0
## 
## locale:
## [1] en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8/C/en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8
## 
## time zone: America/Los_Angeles
## tzcode source: internal
## 
## attached base packages:
## [1] stats     graphics  grDevices utils     datasets  methods   base     
## 
## other attached packages:
##  [1] spdep_1.3-3      spData_2.3.0     scales_1.4.0     geosphere_1.5-18
##  [5] MASS_7.3-60      readxl_1.4.3     sf_1.0-15        lubridate_1.9.4 
##  [9] forcats_1.0.0    stringr_1.5.1    dplyr_1.1.4      purrr_1.0.4     
## [13] readr_2.1.5      tidyr_1.3.1      tibble_3.3.0     ggplot2_3.5.2   
## [17] tidyverse_2.0.0 
## 
## loaded via a namespace (and not attached):
##  [1] gtable_0.3.6       xfun_0.52          bslib_0.6.1        lattice_0.21-9    
##  [5] tzdb_0.4.0         vctrs_0.6.5        tools_4.3.2        generics_0.1.4
